title: March practice-room rent
resolution: Bandmate B did not pay their March share of the practice-room rent; the $120 sent on the payment app was for the February cable and pedalboard reimbursement, not March rent.
notes: Design fixture for the binary classifier catalog (MCP-CAT-001). Not directly runnable by scripts/bot-fixtures/runScenario.js — carries fields the runner does not yet support (semanticEligible, expectedDeterministicComposition, expectedUIState, expectedTimelineBehavior, settlement state, branch ids, timeline lanes, evidence-applicability lifecycle). The fixture's purpose is to make every catalog gap concrete: each eligible move's expectedClassifierSignal list mixes verbatim ids from the current 23-id catalog (src/features/semanticReferee/semanticRefereeTypes.ts) with PROPOSED new ids that this scenario surfaces. The companion research document at docs/roadmap-expansions/2026-05-23-binary-classifier-catalog-design.md lays out the catalog proposal in full. The binary return model is preserved as a hard architectural constraint: the model returns 0/1 signals only; richness comes from deterministic composition, not from model-generated natural language.

alignedWithExistingFixtures.title: human-readable title — matches existing convention
alignedWithExistingFixtures.resolution: the root claim's resolution sentence — matches existing convention
alignedWithExistingFixtures.category: category bucket — existing fixtures use light_civic / sports / everyday / smoke_test_mcp; this card adds design_fixture
alignedWithExistingFixtures.personas: the per-actor list — matches existing convention (alias / side / tone)
alignedWithExistingFixtures.moves: array of moveId-keyed move records — matches existing convention

newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.artifactType: marker that the runner cannot run it as-is
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.roomMetadata: visibility / settlementState / roomType / titleSource — needed by this scenario; existing fixtures have no room-level metadata block
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.participants: richer per-actor record (role descriptor + present/optional flag) — supplements personas
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.semanticRefereeRules: documents the eligibility rules the scenario exercises
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.evidence: first-class evidence object array with applicability lifecycle — the storyboard's evidence object model (docs/ux-storyboards/band-space-rent-private-evidence-argument.md §Evidence object model). Existing fixtures attach evidence inline per move with a label/sourceText; this fixture promotes evidence to a top-level array so applicability can have its own lifecycle.
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.moves[].participantRole / branchId / tim: richer move metadata — UX surface not yet in runner
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.moves[].evidenceAttached / concessions /: structured per-move decomposition — runner does not yet read these
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.moves[].semanticEligible / semanticExemp: the eligibility-rule output per move; runner has no concept of eligibility yet
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.expectedTimeline / expectedSettlement /: design-only sections at the top level
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.currentCatalogCoverage: lists verbatim ids from semanticRefereeTypes.ts that the scenario uses; lists ids the scenario does NOT use (intentional negative coverage)
newFieldsThisFixtureAdds.proposedClassifierNeeds: the catalog gaps surfaced by this scenario, each with a candidate id + rationale + example move

conflictsAvoided.evidence_attached_inline_vs_first_class_: existing fixtures use moves[].evidence = { label, sourceText } as an inline structure. This fixture keeps a per-move evidenceAttached array of evidenceIds AND a top-level evidence array carrying the full evidence objects. The inline structure is preserved (set to null on moves that only reference, not introduce, evidence) so a future runner extension can read the top-level array without breaking on the inline field.
conflictsAvoided.personas_vs_participants: existing fixtures use personas[]. This fixture keeps personas[] for runner-compat AND adds participants[] with richer role information. The two are kept in sync by alias.

roomMetadata.notes: Private from Step 1 of the storyboard. No public observer. Title set by the room creator; a blank title would fall back to a root-claim excerpt per the storyboard, but this scenario uses an explicit title.

participants.notes: Room creator. First participant. Drummer in the band. Believes B did not pay March share.
Named invited respondent. Second participant. Guitarist. Claims the $120 was for March rent.
Bassist. Not present in this walkthrough. May be added as a future participant only if A and B both consent (add-by-consent).

binaryReturnModel.rule: The model returns binary 0/1 classifier signals only. Natural-language output (banners, sidecar copy, timeline labels) is produced by the downstream deterministic composition layer, not by the model.
binaryReturnModel.rationale: Doctrine: AI never decides who is right; richness comes from composition of structural signals, not from model interpretation. Locking the model to binary output makes the safety surface bounded and the composition layer auditable.

rootProclamationExemption.rule: The root proclamation of a debate (the first move in the room, no parent) is SEMANTICALLY EXEMPT — classification does not fire.
rootProclamationExemption.rationale: There is no prior context for the root to engage. responds_to_parent / quote_anchors_parent / answers_clarification all return 0 by definition; the call wastes the token budget and surfaces a degraded banner.

firstRebuttalExemption.rule: The first move by each participant in a debate is SEMANTICALLY EXEMPT — classification does not fire.
firstRebuttalExemption.rationale: Each participant's first move is their opening statement. Author hasn't engaged in any prior structural moves yet, so continuity / movement signals are uninformative.

afterSecondMoveClassificationRule.rule: From each participant's SECOND move onward, classification fires.
afterSecondMoveClassificationRule.rationale: By the second move, the participant has prior context (their own first move plus everything that came in between) and structural signals (continuity, evidence hygiene, narrowing, conceding) become informative.

fullThreadContextRequirement.rule: When classification fires, the request carries the FULL THREAD (all prior moves, each redacted, each labeled with a stable non-identifying author alias like 'A' / 'B' / 'C'). The model judges structural properties against the whole chain, not just the immediate parent.
fullThreadContextRequirement.rationale: A move that responds to a third-level claim or quotes a seventh move cannot be classified accurately on the parent alone. Author aliases preserve speaker identity for continuity reasoning without leaking PII; the system prompt forbids person-labeling regardless.

chimeInExemption.rule: A chime-in participant's FIRST contribution is exempt; classification begins from their second contribution onward — same eligibility curve as the primary participants.
chimeInExemption.rationale: The eligibility rule is participant-scoped; it does not care whether a participant is a primary or a chime-in. The rule applies uniformly.

evidence.notes: The core evidence object. The storyboard makes clear that a payment screenshot proves at most that a payment object exists — never automatically what it was for. Applicability is its own axis.

evidence.noteText: Sending my March rent early so I don't forget.
evidence.claimedApplicability: Supports ev1's applicability to March rent — pre-states intent to pay March rent early
evidence.notes: Closes the evidence debt opened by A's source request at m5. The pre-stated intent (sending rent early) is corroborating, not absolute proof.

evidence.noteText: March rent: $120. Beginning April, rent increases to $140 per share.
evidence.claimedApplicability: Refutes A's m7 introduction of a $20 amount sub-dispute — March was still $120; the increase started in April
evidence.notes: Closes the amount sub-axis A introduced at m7. The scenario could continue from here (A accepts evidence, B confirms, room settles) but the optional confirmation move is the natural close.

moves.body: You still owe your March share of the practice-room rent. The $120 you sent on the payment app was for the February cable and pedalboard reimbursement, not March rent.

expectedDeterministicComposition.rationale: Root proclamation exemption: no classifier call fires; layer-1 deterministic surface renders the opening claim banner without any AI signal.

expectedUIState.sidecar: Opening claim — no prior context to engage.

displayMeta.quoteAnchorCandidate: $120 you sent on the payment app was for the February cable

moves.targetExcerpt: $120 you sent on the payment app was for the February cable
moves.body: I did owe a March share, and the amount was $120. But the payment on March 3 was for practice-room rent. The note said 'practice space.' It was not for the February cable or pedalboard reimbursement.

concessions.text: I did owe a March share.

refutations.text: The payment on March 3 was for practice-room rent. The note said 'practice space.' It was not for the February cable or pedalboard reimbursement.

expectedDeterministicComposition.rationale: B's first move in the room. Eligibility rule exempts the first move per participant. Layer-1 deterministic signals (evidence-attached, concession-list present, refutation present) are visible from the structural fields without an AI call.

expectedUIState.sidecar: B's first response — evidence attached and two concessions filed.

displayMeta.quoteAnchorCandidate: The payment on March 3 was for practice-room rent

moves.body: The March 3 payment note says 'practice space,' but we had already agreed that February's cable and pedalboard reimbursement would be grouped under practice-space expenses. March rent was due March 10, after that payment.

refutations.text: The 'practice space' note covers February reimbursement by prior agreement.
March rent was due March 10, after the March 3 payment.

expectedDeterministicComposition.outputSidecar: A challenges what the payment was FOR, not whether it exists. A cites a prior agreement about February reimbursement and the March 10 due date.
expectedDeterministicComposition.outputRouteSuggestion: mainline (this is a substantive challenge on the same evidence, not a side branch)

expectedUIState.sidecar: Dispute is about which obligation the payment covers, not whether it happened.

moves.expectedTimelineBehavior: node_3_evidence_applicability_challenge_on_mainline; pill on node_2 flips to disputed

moves.body: You're right March rent was due March 10, but I paid early because I knew I would be traveling. I labeled it 'practice space' because that's what it was for.

refutations.text: Paid early because of travel; the label 'practice space' was a description of what the payment was for, not a category code.

expectedDeterministicComposition.outputBanner: Agreed with caveat — alternate interpretation offered
expectedDeterministicComposition.outputSidecar: B accepts the March 10 due date but offers an alternate reading of the payment label and timing.
expectedDeterministicComposition.outputFrictionSuggestion: ask_for_source — the alternate interpretation invites corroboration

expectedUIState.banner: Agreed with caveat — alternate interpretation offered
expectedUIState.sidecar: Conceded the due date; offered alternate interpretation of the payment label.

moves.targetExcerpt: I paid early because I knew I would be traveling
moves.body: Can you show the message where you said you were sending March rent early?

expectedDeterministicComposition.outputSidecar: A explicitly asked for the message backing B's alternate interpretation. Room status: Evidence requested.

moves.expectedTimelineBehavior: node_5_evidence_request_on_mainline; evidence-debt chip appears on node_4

moves.body: Here is the group-chat message I sent: 'Sending my March rent early so I don't forget.' It pre-dates the payment by a few days and shows the intent.

evidence.label: Group-chat excerpt: 'Sending my March rent early so I don't forget.'
evidence.sourceText: Sending my March rent early so I don't forget.

expectedDeterministicComposition.outputSidecar: B supplied the group-chat excerpt. Evidence pill on m2 strengthens; ev1 applicability moves toward supported.

moves.expectedTimelineBehavior: node_6_evidence_supply_on_mainline; evidence-debt chip on node_4 flips to resolved; pill on node_2 shows stronger state

moves.body: I concede the $120 payment was intended for March rent and that I misread the payment timing. But I still think you owe $20 because the rent went up to $140 starting March.

concessions.text: The $120 payment was intended for March rent.

refutations.text: Rent went up to $140 starting March; you still owe $20.

expectedDeterministicComposition.outputSidecar: Applicability resolved. New sub-axis opened: was March rent $120 or $140?
expectedDeterministicComposition.outputRouteSuggestion: mainline (the amount sub-axis is on the same mainline; opening a side branch would fragment the resolution)
expectedDeterministicComposition.outputFrictionSuggestion: ask_for_source — the new $140 claim needs corroboration

expectedUIState.sidecar: ev1 applicability accepted; live dispute narrows to the amount.

moves.expectedTimelineBehavior: node_7_concession_with_new_sub_axis on mainline; ev1 pill flips to accepted

moves.targetExcerpt: rent went up to $140 starting March
moves.body: The rent increase started in April. March was still $120. The landlord's message confirms it: 'March rent: $120. Beginning April, rent increases to $140 per share.'

refutations.text: Rent increase started in April, not March; landlord message confirms.

expectedDeterministicComposition.outputBanner: Sub-dispute evidence supplied; ready for synthesis
expectedDeterministicComposition.outputSidecar: B supplies landlord message confirming March rent was still $120; the $20 sub-claim is refuted with primary-source evidence. Room may settle.

expectedUIState.banner: Sub-dispute evidence supplied; ready for synthesis

mainlineProgression: m2 (B's evidence-backed rebuttal with concessions, ev1 pill attached)
m3 (A's evidence applicability challenge, ev1 pill flips to disputed)
m5 (A's ask for source, evidence-debt chip on m4)
m6 (B's evidence supply, ev2 pill attached, debt chip resolves, ev1 pill strengthens)
m7 (A's concession + new sub-axis on amount, ev1 pill accepts)
m8 (B's evidence-backed rebuttal on sub-axis, ev3 pill attached)

branches.renderingLane: shares lane_0_mainline (the storyboard's mainline progression is sequential; branch_id is a logical group, not a separate visual lane)
branches.label: Applicability of ev1

expectedTimeline.settlementCollapseTrigger: A accepts ev3 and writes a summary (the next move beyond the scope of this fixture's 8 moves; described under expectedSettlement)

finalLockedState.lockingPolicy: private_room_locks_as_settled — grey, read-only; reading and referencing by authorized participants remain

evidenceFinalStates.ev3: attached_unchallenged (or attached_accepted after A's settlement move)

expectedSettlement.transitionTrigger: A taps Accept evidence on ev3, writes a settlement summary, B taps Confirm resolution.
expectedSettlement.settlementSummaryAllowed: Resolved: March was paid. I confused the February reimbursement and the April rent increase with March.
expectedSettlement.notes: The storyboard makes clear that even at settlement, the word is 'settled' — never 'proven', 'true', 'case closed'. The settlement summary is user-written; the system never asserts truth.

currentCatalogCoverage.coverageNotes: 9 of 23 catalog ids are exercised positively. 14 are unused. This is by scenario design — the band-space-rent argument is a structured, calm, evidence-driven dispute between two participants. Unused ids cover scenarios this scenario doesn't model (satire, popularity appeals, person-shifts, hot takes, side-branch routing). The scenario is intentionally narrow to surface evidence and applicability gaps; a future fixture should exercise the other 14 ids.

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move challenge what an attached evidence object COVERS rather than whether the evidence exists?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: The current catalog has 'evidence_supports_claim' (does the evidence support THIS claim) but no signal for 'the evidence exists but is being applied to the wrong obligation'. Applicability is a distinct axis from existence per the storyboard's evidence object model.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m3 — A says the $120 payment exists but was for February reimbursement, not March rent.

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move cite a prior agreement between the participants that bears on the current dispute?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: Two-party arguments often turn on a previously-established convention (here: that February reimbursement was 'practice-space' coded). The catalog has no signal for this.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m3 — 'we had already agreed that February's cable and pedalboard reimbursement would be grouped under practice-space expenses.'

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move cite a specific date, timeline, or temporal boundary that constrains the dispute?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: Disputes over WHEN something happened or applied are common; the catalog handles evidence presence and source-chain hygiene but not temporal anchoring as a distinct signal.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m3 — 'March rent was due March 10, after that payment.'

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move accept some specific point raised by the parent while qualifying or restricting the acceptance?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: The catalog has 'concedes_narrow_point' (full narrow concession). The storyboard's 'Agree with caveat' is a distinct response choice that lives between full concession and full rebuttal. Reviewers should consider whether this is a merge with 'concedes_narrow_point' or a new id.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m4 — 'You're right March rent was due March 10, but I paid early because of travel.'

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move offer an alternative reading of an existing artifact (note text, date, label) that the parent treated as fixed?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: Distinct from 'requests_clarification' (asking what the parent meant) and from 'provides_evidence' (adding new evidence). The move re-frames an existing artifact's interpretation.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m4 — B re-interprets the payment note 'practice space' as a description, not a category code.

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move open a structured evidence-debt marker (e.g. an Ask for source request that the system tracks)?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: Distinct from generic 'asks_for_evidence'. The catalog should track the structured-request action separately from the informal one because the deterministic ledger needs to open a tracked debt object.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m5 — A taps 'Ask for source' and writes a specific request.

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move respond to an open evidence-debt marker with the requested source or quote?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: The deterministic ledger needs a signal that closes a debt; 'provides_evidence' is too broad (it fires on any evidence attachment).
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m6 — B supplies the group-chat excerpt that A requested at m5.

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move attach a document (chat excerpt, message, receipt) that corroborates a prior claim already on the timeline rather than introducing a new primary claim?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: Differentiates corroborating evidence from primary evidence. The deterministic layer treats them differently (corroborating strengthens an existing pill; primary creates a new one).
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m6 — group-chat excerpt corroborates the timing of ev1; m8 — landlord message corroborates the amount.

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move open a new, more specific sub-dispute on the SAME mainline rather than continuing the parent's axis?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: Distinct from 'introduces_new_issue' (which signals a wholly new debatable issue, typically a candidate for a side branch). Sub-axis stays on the mainline; the catalog lacks this finer distinction.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m7 — A concedes applicability and opens a new sub-axis on amount ($20).

proposedClassifierNeeds.binarySignal: Does this move pair a concession on one axis with a new dispute on a different axis?
proposedClassifierNeeds.rationaleForAddition: A common move shape in real disputes; the catalog handles concession but not the paired concede-and-open pattern. Could be a composition rule rather than a new id; flagged for reviewer.
proposedClassifierNeeds.exampleMoveInScenario: m7 — A concedes applicability AND opens the amount sub-dispute in the same move.

compositionRules.outputSidecar: Dispute is about which obligation the payment covers, not whether it happened. Prior agreement cited.

compositionRules.outputBanner: Agreed with caveat — alternate interpretation offered
compositionRules.outputSidecar: Conceded the specific point; offered an alternate interpretation of an existing artifact.
Prior dispute resolved; live dispute narrows to the new sub-axis.

compositionRules.outputBanner: Sub-dispute evidence supplied; ready for synthesis

compositionRules.outputSidecar: Opening claim — no prior context to engage.
Author's first move — eligibility rule exempts the move from classification.

reasonCodeSafetyNotes.context: The current packet schema includes a reasonCode string per classifier. The SMOKE-FIX-001 investigation surfaced that this field is at risk of containing banned tokens (false, true, correct, incorrect, wrong) since classifiers commonly use those tokens in plausible reason codes (e.g. false_premise, true_to_topic).
designConstraintsForReasonCode: No verdict tokens — never includes winner / loser / won / lost / right / wrong / true / false / correct / incorrect / proven / defeated.
No person labels — never includes liar / lying / dishonest / manipulative / troll / propagandist / extremist / stupid / idiot / dumb / smart or the phrase 'bad faith'.
No truth claims — describes the move's STRUCTURE, never asserts the move IS factual / IS wrong / IS misleading.

Snake_case bounded vocabulary — drawn from the six MCP-002 reason-code families (parent_continuity / branch_routing / evidence / source_chain / movement / mode_fit / friction / banner) per src/features/semanticReferee/semanticRefereeTypes.ts.

Whitelist-by-family — the validator should accept reasonCode only if it matches /^(parent_continuity|branch_routing|evidence|source_chain|movement|mode_fit|friction|banner)_[a-z0-9_]+$/ AND none of its segments are in the verdict/person ban-list.
